In the proposed scenario the third infall can be the result of the dynamical interaction between the Milky Way and Sagittarius, as suggested by lara2020 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy as a possible massive external perturber, citing simulations where Sgr can or cannot reproduce Milky Way vertical waves and Radcliffe Wave alignment.

quotes.quote: Various simulations have reproduced the Milky Way's spiral arms and vertical oscillations with the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy (Sgr), under the constraint that the initial mass of Sgr is more massive than previously assumed Laporte_2019, Bland_Hawthorn_2019 . In contrast, Bennett_2021 have argued through a suite of N-body simulations that Sgr could not have produced the vertical waves in Bennett_2019 .

Running N-body simulations of a satellite as massive as the Sgr dwarf galaxy impacting the galactic disk, Thula_2021 conclude that the resultant density wave aligns with the Radcliffe Wave, suggesting the wave may be the result of an external perturber.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Sagittarius Dwarf Galaxy as a possible Milky Way disk perturber, while noting simulations where Sagittarius alone cannot explain the Gaia snail or vertical waves.

quotes.quote: Such effects may be due to the last impact of the Sagittarius Dwarf Galaxy, modulated by the influence from the Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC) laportefluence .

For context, we recall that Bennett2022Exploring_Sgr-MW, Bennett2021snailisnotsgr contend that the Sagittarius impact picture alone cannot reproduce either the Gaia snail or the vertical waves in simulations. Moreover, Gandhi2022snailacrossscales show that the current passage of the Sagittarius Dwarf has already had its greatest effect while below the disk, and is likely to have made a much smaller effect when it crosses into the North, due to a larger average galactocentric radius.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy as a close Milky Way satellite whose orbit is compatible with the Snail timing, while prior simulations require a larger effective perturber mass than its current mass.

quotes.quote: The closest luminous satellites of the Milky Way and hence its strongest current external perturbers are the Sagittarius (Sgr) dwarf galaxy ibata_1994 and the Large Magellanic Cloud, which have masses of 4 10^8 M_ vasiliev_belokurov_2020 and 1.4 10^ 11 M_ erkal_2019, vasiliev_2021 respectively and distances from the Sun of 25 kpc and 50 kpc ibata_1997, pietrzynski_2013 .

The derived encounter time is consistent with the orbit of the Sgr dwarf galaxy, although with large uncertainties e.g., antoja_2018 . Simulations exploring this scenario laporte_2018_sgr,laporte_2019_sgrfootprint,bland-hawthorn_2019, bennett_bovy_2021,hunt_2021, bennett_2022, gandhi_2022_snail , ranging from test-particle simulations to idealized N-body simulations to cosmological simulations, have shown that the perturber mass necessary to reproduce the amplitude of the observed Snail is larger than the current Sgr mass by factors of a few: the present-day dynamical mass of the Sgr dwarf is 4 10^8 M_ vasiliev_belokurov_2020 , whereas these simulations, including the stripping of the dwarf as it falls in, require a present-day mass of 10^ 9 - 3 10^ 10 M_ or very rapid mass loss in the recent past.

sources.summary: The review describes Sagittarius as a massive dwarf spheroidal with process elements and enhanced Eu/Mg, indicating a relatively large delayed-source contribution compared with Sculptor.

quotes.quote: Sculptor, Fornax, and Sagittarius are sufficiently massive that they should be dominated by chemical evolution rather than enrichment. Sculptor is one of the best-studied dwarf galaxies with a very simple star formation history and smooth process chemical evolution Duggan2018, Hill2019, Skuladottir2019, Molero2021, delosReyes2022 . There is still debate about the relative importance of prompt vs delayed process sources in Sculptor, but it clearly has reached the point of continuous chemical evolution with [ Eu/Mg ] 0 . Fornax Letarte2010, Lemasle2014 and Sagittarius Bonifacio2000,McWilliam2013,Hansen2018 are two of the more massive dwarf spheroidal galaxies with process elements, both with enhanced [ Eu/Mg ] 0.4 . These indicate that Fornax and Sagittarius have a relatively large contribution from delayed sources of process elements compared to Sculptor, which matches the difference in star formation durations seen in the bottom panel of Figure fig:dwarfprop . Note that there is clearly some inhomogeneous process enrichment in Fornax, as some of its most metal-rich stars have extreme Eu enhancements Reichert2021 .

sources.summary: The paper links a 6 Gyr Milky Way star-formation break to the first infall of the Sgr dwarf galaxy, arguing that gas accretion, disk reorganization, bar-triggered migration, and later pericentric passages may follow.

quotes.quote: This event is likely the first infall of the Sgr dwarf galaxy, and we hypothesise, in agreement with results of cosmological simulations, that it was accompanied by a substantial amount of gas accretion which led to an important chemical and kinematic reorganisation of the disk, accompanied by further disk growth. Stellar migration to the solar radius could possibly be enhanced at this time by a growing bar which formation could have been triggered by the interaction with Sgr itself, and thus the presence of stellar populations with such different metallicity.

The final, more vigorous period of star formation since 4 Gyr ago may as well hold some relation with further Sgr pericentric passages (as hinted by some gas dilution between 3 and 1.5 Gyr ago) and converges to a mainly local population of metallicity very close to solar.

sources.summary: The paper compares Sagittarius to disrupted low-surface-brightness satellite remnants, citing its extreme ellipticity and arguing that nearby circular-orbit LSB dwarfs can produce similar density enhancements.

quotes.quote: Ellipticities of LG dSphs range from the extreme value of 0.8 in Sagittarius and 0.65 for Ursa Minor to 0.2-0.3 for Sextans, with the average being around 0.4 (Mateo 1998).

Sagittarius might be the nearly-disrupted remnant of such a satellite: what remains of the central part of the LSB satellite, in run L19 and L20, is recognizable as a density enhancement along the streams for a few Gyr and has a maximum elongation b/a=0.2 , similar to that inferred for Sagittarius (Mateo 1998).

LSB satellites on circular orbits very close to the Milky Way can even disrupt due to their large, low density disks and will produce long-lasting density enhancements that resemble the dwarf spheroidal Sagittarius.

sources.summary: The paper links Terzan 7 to the Sgr dSph and says its low alpha-element and nickel ratios match field stars and clusters associated with that system.

quotes.quote: We study the chemical composition of 5 giant stars in the globular cluster Terzan 7 associated with the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy ( Sgr dSph ), based on high resolution UVES-VLT spectra.
We also show that this cluster displays the same low element to iron ratio found in Sgr dSph field stars of similar metallicity, as well as the same low Ni/Fe ratio.

These chemical signatures are characteristic of the Sgr dSph system, and appear to be shared both by the globular cluster Pal 12 , which was most likely stripped from Sgr by tidal interaction, and by Pal 5 , which may also have belonged in the past to the Sgr dSph system.
